questions.question: Why is a rooted Android device preferred over an emulator for certain aspects of mobile security testing? (2-3 marks)
expectedPoints: Provides true hardware and sensor interaction
Allows full access to the app's sandboxed data directory via `adb`
Necessary for some advanced hooking and debugging tools

questions.question: Compare and contrast SAST and DAST tools for mobile application security. Include in your answer the phase of the SDLC they are typically used, their key advantages, and one example tool for each. (5-10 marks)
expectedPoints: Definition: SAST = static code analysis, DAST = testing running application.
SDLC Phase: SAST used early in development/CI; DAST used later on test builds.
Advantage: SAST finds code flaws early; DAST finds runtime configuration issues.
Advantage: SAST has full code coverage; DAST requires no source code.
Disadvantage: SAST can yield false positives; DAST can have limited coverage.
Example SAST Tool: MobSF, SonarQube, Checkmarx.
Example DAST Tool: OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite, MobSF Dynamic Analyzer.
